class.name: FmodAudioEffectEQ
class.brief_description: Base class for multi-band equalizer audio effects.
class.description: FmodAudioEffectEQ is a base class for multi-band equalizer audio effects using FMOD's MULTIBAND_EQ DSP. It provides flexible frequency equalization with support for multiple bands, each with independent gain, frequency, Q value, and filter type. The effect supports three preset configurations: 6-band, 10-band, and 21-band equalizers. Each band uses a peaking filter by default, but can be configured with other filter types including low-pass, high-pass, low-shelf, high-shelf, and more. To apply this effect to an audio bus, use [method apply_to] and pass a [FmodChannelGroup].

method.name: get_band_count
method.description: Returns the number of bands in this equalizer, which depends on the preset (6, 10, or 21).

method.name: get_band_filter_type
method.description: Returns the filter type for the specified band. See [enum FilterType] for possible values.

method.name: get_band_frequency
method.description: Returns the center frequency (in Hz) for the specified band.

method.name: get_band_gain_db
method.description: Returns the gain (in dB) for the specified band. The value ranges from -60 dB to +24 dB.

method.name: get_band_q
method.description: Returns the Q value (quality factor) for the specified band. Higher Q values result in a narrower bandwidth.

method.name: set_band_filter_type
method.description: Sets the filter type for the specified band. See [enum FilterType] for available filter types.

method.name: set_band_frequency
method.description: Sets the center frequency (in Hz) for the specified band. Valid range is 10 Hz to 22000 Hz.

method.name: set_band_gain_db
method.description: Sets the gain (in dB) for the specified band. The value is clamped to the range -60 dB to +24 dB.

method.name: set_band_q
method.description: Sets the Q value (quality factor) for the specified band. Valid range is 0.01 to 10.0. Higher values create a narrower, more selective filter.

members.member: The EQ preset determining the number of bands. Use [constant PRESET_6_BANDS], [constant PRESET_10_BANDS], or [constant PRESET_21_BANDS]. Changing this will reinitialize all bands with default values.

constants.constant: Filter is disabled for this band.
Low-shelf filter for boosting or cutting low frequencies.
High-shelf filter for boosting or cutting high frequencies.
Peaking EQ filter for boosting or cutting a specific frequency range. This is the default filter type for EQ bands.
Band-pass filter that allows only a specific frequency range to pass.
Notch filter that removes a specific frequency.
All-pass filter that passes all frequencies but changes the phase.
